title: Lappa tenella

It belongs to the tribe Cardueae, a group of plants that include thistles, artichokes, and cardoons. Lappa tenella is further classified in the genus Lappa, which is a small genus of plants native to Europe and Asia. The species is known to produce yellow flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory properties. Lappa tenella is considered a rare and local plant species.

intro: Lappa tenella, also known as Notopterygium forbesii, is a type of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae, commonly found in the mountainous regions of China and other parts of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties.
